evidence: Person missing recommended: jobTitle, sameAs, hasCredential
FAQPage schema has 7 Questions but page shows ~14 FAQ pairs

No TL;DR block (expected

or paragraph starting with 'TL;DR:')
No visible 'Last updated' / 'Last reviewed' / 'Next review' stamp on page (schema dateModified alone isn't enough for users or AI)
Author has no sameAs URLs (should link LinkedIn + at least one other profile)
Author has no jobTitle or description — credentials not stated
No first-party data signals ("we tested", "our data shows", "we analyzed"). Add at least one concrete observation
Only 3/4 human signals present (author+LinkedIn=false, first-party data=false, original visual=true, 3+ citations=true). High AI-content-flag risk
Specific human voice vs committee 3/10 — Reads like branded editorial content written by consensus, with stock phrases like 'evidence-backed' and 'unique UAE lifestyle.'
Genuine opinions vs safe claims 4/10 — Mostly safe, hedged claims. A couple of opinionated lines exist ('anyone telling you to grind...is selling something') but they're drowned out.
Unexpected phrasings 5/10 — Mostly predictable corporate-wellness phrasing, though 'Your willpower expires in February; your calendar doesn't' is genuinely good.
Specific citations 6/10 — Has numbers, AED prices, DHA rules—but references are vague bracket tags with no authors, dates, or URLs; stats feel fabricated.
Point of view vs balanced survey 4/10 — Mostly neutral institutional summary; rare mild stances are immediately softened.
Quotable sentence 2/10 — Nothing memorable; everything is reportage-flat.
Intro earns attention 3/10 — Opens with navigation crumbs, TL;DR, byline, and a vague claim before the genuinely hooky 64% statistic buried paragraphs down.

intro_hook 6/10 — The TL;DR block and 'Quick answer' section front-load good material, but by the time the actual body prose begins, the first sentence is partially redundant with what was already stated twice above it. The hook loses urgency because it's the third time the reader sees the 64% stat. The sentence is salvageable but needs to land as if it's the first time the reader is hearing it, not a recap. Score docked for the structural repetition undermining the hook's punch, not for the sentence quality in isolation.
